potential_missing.source_sentence: If they log in, they can continue with different roles.
If the person is an admin, they can manage user profiles by adding, editing, or removing them.

potential_hallucinations.requirement: The system shall require a person to either create an account or log in.
The system shall enable admins to review articles and decide on their publication or rejection.
The system shall permit content creators to write articles and send them for editing.
The system shall have editors review articles from content creators and pass them for approval.
The system shall restrict access if a login attempt is invalid.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If the account is created, they may continue with admin-related actions. If they log in, they can continue with different roles.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall differentiate between user roles based on whether an account was created or a login was performed.

missing_requirements.evidence_quote: If the person is working on content, they can write an article and send it for editing. The editor reviews the article and passes it forward for approval.
missing_requirements.suggested_requirement: The system shall allow users to work on content by writing articles and sending them for editing.

hallucinations.rewritten_requirement: The system shall make published articles available for user reading.
hallucinations.reason: The source text mentions that once an article is published, users can read it. However, the rewritten requirement states 'make published articles available,' which implies a different action.

duplicates.requirement_a: The system shall allow admins to add, edit, or remove user profiles.
duplicates.requirement_b: The system shall enable admins to review articles and decide on their publication or rejection.
duplicates.reason: Both requirements are specific actions that admins can perform but are listed separately.

ambiguities.rewritten_requirement: The system shall permit content creators to write articles and send them for editing.
ambiguities.reason: It is not clear if 'content creators' refers to a specific role or any user who can create content. The source text does not provide this clarity.

prompt:
You are a strict requirements validator.
Compare rewritten requirements against the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "missing_requirements": [
    {
      "evidence_quote": "string",
      "suggested_requirement": "The system shall ..."
    }
  ],
  "hallucinations": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "duplicates": [
    {
      "requirement_a": "string",
      "requirement_b": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "ambiguities": [
    {
      "rewritten_requirement": "string",
      "reason": "string"
    }
  ],
  "overall_assessment": "pass|needs_review"
}

Source text:
<<<
A person starts by either creating an account or logging in.

If the account is created, they may continue with admin-related actions. If they log in, they can continue with different roles.

If the person is an admin, they can manage user profiles by adding, editing, or removing them. They can also review articles and decide whether to publish them or reject them.

If the person is working on content, they can write an article and send it for editing. The editor reviews the article and passes it forward for approval.

If the login is not valid, the person is not allowed to continue.

Once an article is published, users can read it. While reading, they can continue reading more articles.

After reading, the user can either search for more articles or give feedback.

If feedback is given or the user finishes searching, it ends.
>>>

Rewritten requirements JSON:
{
  "rewritten_requirements": [
    "The system shall require a person to either create an account or log in.",
    "The system shall allow admins to add, edit, or remove user profiles.",
    "The system shall enable admins to review articles and decide on their publication or rejection.",
    "The system shall permit content creators to write articles and send them for editing.",
    "The system shall have editors review articles from content creators and pass them for approval.",
    "The system shall restrict access if a login attempt is invalid.",
    "The system shall make published articles available for user reading.",
    "The system shall allow users to search for more articles during their reading session.",
    "The system shall enable users to provide feedback after reading articles.",
    "The system shall end when users complete their search or provide feedback."
  ]
}
